canonical_rules.requirement: CLI code remains thin and delegates validation or orchestration to typed modules.
canonical_rules.failure_mode: command handler performs product inference or lock mutation directly

canonical_rules.requirement: Adapters preserve source hashes and emit normalization evidence.
canonical_rules.failure_mode: adapter emits downstream product semantics without source intake

canonical_rules.requirement: Program DAG nodes and edges are deterministic and acyclic.
canonical_rules.failure_mode: replay produces different node ids or execution order

canonical_rules.requirement: Commands stop at review, lock, release, and unresolved invalidation gates.
canonical_rules.failure_mode: command silently creates a decision, lock, promotion, package, or release

canonical_rules.requirement: Every command emits structured status with exit code and findings.
canonical_rules.failure_mode: command only writes human text or hides failure evidence

canonical_rules.requirement: Integration layer cannot add business contracts absent from source and locked stage evidence.
canonical_rules.failure_mode: adapter or driver creates product capability from formatting or CLI flags
